failingTest: N/A — behavioral issue: fabricated identical scores across 213 Swift units because `lint_errors` metric is never collected (parent issue #21)
fileMap: cmd/certify/init_cmd.go — modify — remove dead lint-clean rule block (lines 709-716) from swiftStandardPolicy constant; no collector exists to supply the lint_errors metric
fixApproach: Delete the 8-line `lint-clean` rule block (lines 709–716 + trailing blank) from the `swiftStandardPolicy` constant. This rule references `metric: lint_errors` but no SwiftLint collector exists anywhere in the codebase, so the metric is never populated — causing every Swift unit to trigger "missing evidence" violations and receive identical fabricated grades. Removal is preferred over comment-wrapping because the suggested comment references `language_tiers.go` which does not exist. The remaining `swiftStandardPolicy` will have 3 live rules: `test-pass`, `no-todos`, and `max-complexity`. No test directly inspects Swift policy content, so no test changes are needed.
steps: Open cmd/certify/init_cmd.go and locate the swiftStandardPolicy constant (around line 704)
Delete the 8-line lint-clean rule block (7 YAML lines + 1 trailing blank line) from within the rules: section
Verify the resulting swiftStandardPolicy has 3 rules: test-pass, no-todos, max-complexity
Run go build -o build/bin/certify ./cmd/certify/ to confirm clean compilation
Run go test ./... -count=1 to confirm no tests break
Run a full-text search for 'SwiftLint' across .go files to confirm zero remaining references
summary: Remove the dead SwiftLint `lint-clean` rule (8 lines) from the `swiftStandardPolicy` constant in `cmd/certify/init_cmd.go` — no collector exists to evaluate it, causing fabricated grades for Swift repositories.
